last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.title: Invariant-call route
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
moves.needs_frame: Postcondition shape only — no `={...}` equality frame. Build the `={...}` part from `application_context.write_map` (per candidate field: who writes it — the read-only/preserved fields are safe to equate), then add your coupling before this is a usable invariant.

call_sites.statement: b <@ RCPAa(RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A), QueryBounder( RCPAa( RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A)), CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.RCPA_Wrap(Random)).O').distinguish()
b <@ RCPAa(RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A), QueryBounder( RCPAa( RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A)), CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.RCPA_Wrap( CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.Ideal)).O').distinguish()

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier; call sites are visible at the frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The first visible statements have different heads; align or consume setup statements before using a symmetric branch/loop/call tactic.

rows.proof_read: These are setup statements before the current frontier; preserve the proof-relevant initialized state in later invariants, seq cuts, or calls.

rows.left: b <@ RCPAa(RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A), QueryBounder( RCPAa( RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A)), CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.RCPA_Wrap(Random)).O').distinguish()
rows.right: b <@ RCPAa(RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A), QueryBounder( RCPAa( RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A)), CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.RCPA_Wrap( CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.Ideal)).O').distinguish()
rows.proof_read: Both sides expose the same call procedure; a named equiv call or an invariant call may apply at this frontier.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 1 1 / wp -1 -1 absorbs the straight-line setup (1 left, 1 right) before the next frontier

left.statement: b <@ RCPAa(RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A), QueryBounder( RCPAa( RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A)), CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.RCPA_Wrap(Random)).O').distinguish()

right.statement: b <@ RCPAa(RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A), QueryBounder( RCPAa( RCPA_WUF_RCPA.RCPAa(MAC, A)), CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.RCPA_Wrap( CBCa.SKEa.RCPA.Ideal)).O').distinguish()

application_context.how_to_use: Use this section when constructing the concrete invariant inside `call (_: ...)`. This section is not a tactic. It is a checklist for constructing the concrete invariant before using `probe_tactic` or `commit_tactic`.

inspect_if_unsure.why: After you draft a concrete invariant, this preview shows the obligations it creates and can reveal missing facts or extra conjuncts that make the proof harder.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis layer is call_site with live procedure calls, but no callable lemma handle is currently attached. Additional bridge/call lemma context may be relevant before changing this frontier by global inlining.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The visible frontier contains call sites or named equiv handles may apply.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are at an abstract-adversary `call (_: <inv>)` and want the mechanical glob frame of the invariant before adding your own semantic conjuncts.
ask_manager_for.returns: the mechanical `={glob ...}` frame of the call invariant (shared abstract oracle modules, daemon-confirmed to apply and spawn the obligations). When a named coupling predicate fits the in-scope state it ALSO surfaces a field-threaded carrier (`inv … s1{1} s2{2}`), plus candidate inductive side-conditions (`dom ⊆ used-set`) for any random oracle the call queries. All revisable starting points — confirm the coupling and add/adjust the semantic conjuncts (key/state correspondences, set-membership).

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: A tactic has multiple EasyCrypt argument forms.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: The frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch or call tactics.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

ask_manager_for.use_when: LHS/RHS statement order may need swap/alignment context.
ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier
